title: Conversation failures auto-file draft tickets: close the notice→ticket loop
current_state: /ticket-mine already mines candidates read-only, but a human must run it and hand-write the ticket. Pi 'noticing' its own failures (pillar 2) still depends on Ash noticing first.
target_state: A scheduled pass (piggyback the T-202 nightly) runs the miner over logs/turns.jsonl + evolution.jsonl since last run; candidates above a confidence threshold are written as FULL ticket JSONs into tickets/drafts/ (NOT open/ — drafts need Ash's promote). Telegram summary lists new drafts one line each. Promotion = Ash moves the file or says 'promote T-draft-N'.
migration_plan: 1. tickets/drafts/ directory; sprint.py and refresh_pi.py must IGNORE it (drafts are not queue).
2. Miner gains --emit-drafts: candidate → ticket JSON via router tier='cheap' (Groq writes the draft from the evidence rows).
3. Dedup against open/ + closed/ + drafts/ titles (the L2-dedup pattern) so the same failure doesn't refile nightly.
4. Tests: candidate→draft shape valid, dedup suppresses refiles, drafts dir invisible to sprint list_open_tickets (extend test_sprint_isolation).
risk_notes: Drafts-not-open is the human-in-the-loop boundary — auto-filing into open/ would let Pi feed its own autonomous queue, which couples two autonomy systems before either is trusted. Keep the gate.
